task_completion.description: Verify the agent opens the source text file to read the story.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or viewer open on the screen displaying the contents of 'sherlock_holmes.txt' or the text of 'A Scandal in Bohemia'.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent opens the Sugar Write activity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Sugar Write application interface open and active on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify the document contains the four exact required headings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the exact text: 'Case Summary', 'Characters', 'Clues and Deductions', and 'Conclusion' formatted as headings or distinct section titles within the Write document.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Characters' section lists at least 4 characters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for at least four distinct characters (e.g., Holmes, Watson, Irene Adler, King of Bohemia) listed under the 'Characters' heading.

task_completion.description: Verify the document contains relevant case details and deductions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for story-specific terms (e.g., 'photograph', 'disguise', 'clergyman', 'Briony Lodge') written in the summary, deductions, and conclusion sections.

task_completion.description: Verify the document is saved as an ODT file to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a save dialog, file manager, or application title bar indicating the file is being saved as 'case_analysis.odt' in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Sugar Write GUI to create the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for active typing and interaction within the Sugar Write application. The agent should not be using terminal commands (like echo or python scripts) to bypass the GUI and generate the file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent generated an actual summary and didn't just copy the prompt.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for original sentences describing the plot and characters, rather than a direct copy-paste of the task description text.

integrity.description: Verify the file is saved properly through the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the use of the application's native save/export functionality, ensuring it's not just a plain text file renamed with an .odt extension via the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected output file: /home/ga/Documents/case_analysis.odt. Source text: /home/ga/Documents/sherlock_holmes.txt. Required exact headings: 'Case Summary', 'Characters', 'Clues and Deductions', 'Conclusion'. Expected terms to look for in the text: Holmes, Watson, Adler, Irene, King, Bohemia, photograph, disguise, clergyman, Briony Lodge.
